title: Architectural separation of git→FUSE sync into single-purpose dome-mirror agent; restore workspace-sync to its original unidirectional FUSE→git identity

problem.summary: After 6 successive iterations attempting to make workspace-sync reliably invoke its Step 4c (git→FUSE propagation helper), the cumulative success rate ceiling stabilized at ~14% (1 sentinel in 7 overnight cycles post-PROP-073). The failure mode was identical across all 6 iterations: the LLM agent (Haiku, later confirmed under Sonnet) pattern-matched the unfamiliar git→FUSE half as decorative scaffolding and silently dropped it, regardless of where in the prompt it was placed, how it was framed, or what self-attestation backstops were in place.

iteration_history.approach: extract Step 4c logic into monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js so the in-prompt invocation is 3 lines instead of ~140; helper always writes exactly one sentinel so 'no-op' is distinguishable from 'didn't run'
iteration_history.result: post-deploy sentinel rate ~0 across multiple cycles; LLM still skipped the 3-line invocation

iteration_history.approach: collapse Step 4a + Step 4c + Step 4b + Cleanup into ONE bash tool call (so trailing-block skip mode is structurally impossible)
iteration_history.result: 7/7 cycles 09:33→16:09Z produced zero sync-workspace-runs sentinels while Steps 4a + 4b ran reliably in all 7 (run-reports visible). MIDDLE-position skip remained even within a single bash block

iteration_history.approach: hoist Step 4c to FIRST position inside the collapsed block (immediately after trap setup, before Step 4a's commit). Reasoning: LLM agents reliably execute FIRST + LAST positions but drop MIDDLE
iteration_history.result: post-hoist cycles still produced mis-titled commits 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel: ...' that contained ZERO sync-workspace-runs sentinels (Step 3 universal-pusher rescues mis-attributed). Sentinel rate ~14%

iteration_history.approach: reframe prompt opening from 'You are the Workspace Sync agent' to 'You are the bidirectional FUSE↔git syncer'; both halves are core, every cycle. Hypothesis: self-conception framing would override pattern-rejection
iteration_history.result: no measurable improvement; pattern-recognition over the prompt body filtered the git→FUSE half regardless of opening framing

iteration_history.approach: stage-delta gating + AGENT_NOTES self-attestation backstop + filename-ISO parse to fix the false-positive SENTINEL_COUNT mtime heuristic + absolute write-path log in helper. Three sub-fixes to prevent the mis-titled-commit failure mode + give detective signal when Step 4c silently skipped
iteration_history.result: stage-delta gating DID work for preventing mis-titled commits (defense-in-depth detection layers ARE effective); but preventive prompt-design fix for the underlying pattern-rejection failure did not. Post-PROP-073 sentinel rate: 1/7 = ~14%

iteration_history.approach: architectural separation — extract Step 4c into its own dome-mirror scheduled agent with a single-action prompt; restore workspace-sync to its original FUSE→git-only identity

problem.root_cause_finding: Agent self-conception filters out unfamiliar actions regardless of prompt design. workspace-sync's months-long established identity as a FUSE→git pusher is fundamentally incompatible with adding a git→FUSE half. Sonnet (and likely Haiku) filter the unfamiliar action out via pattern-recognition over the prompt; preventive prompt-design fixes (relocation, reframing, self-attestation) all failed because they operate on the wrong layer.
problem.smoking_gun_commit: fb52feb8 (2026-05-31T21:20Z) — titled 'PROP-066 step4c sentinel' but contained 13 unrelated files from Step 3 universal-pusher rescues. Proved the pattern-rejection failure mode: the LLM ran the surrounding commit-and-push wrapper but skipped the actual `node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js` invocation in between, so when the wrapper looked for sentinels there were none, but git diff --cached --quiet was false (Step 3 had already staged its files), so the wrapper committed Step 3's work under Step 4c's title.

solution.summary: Extract the git→FUSE sync into a new, single-purpose dome-mirror scheduled agent. When the only action in the prompt IS the helper invocation, pattern-matching cannot filter it out. workspace-sync returns to its original unidirectional identity (FUSE→git only), which Sonnet/Haiku has been reliably executing for months.

new_agent.name: dome-mirror
new_agent.single_action: Run monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js; commit any sentinel it wrote; push; cleanup
new_agent.scheduling_offset_rationale: dome-mirror at :00 runs BEFORE workspace-sync at :05 (or :10). dome-mirror updates FUSE from git → workspace-sync's smart_copy then compares fresh FUSE vs clone. If workspace-sync ran first with stale FUSE, smart_copy could revert recent git commits via the mtime-guard backfire. Recommended cron: dome-mirror `0 * * * *` + workspace-sync `5 * * * *` (5-min offset).

helper_script_disposition.decision: REUSE as-is for v1. Optional cosmetic rename to dome-mirror-sync.js deferred (creates rebase noise; helper is proven correct when invoked)

workspace_sync_changes.opening_reframe: Title reverted from 'Bidirectional FUSE↔git syncer' to 'FUSE→git pusher'. Body reverted from 'You are the bidirectional FUSE↔git syncer' to 'You are the FUSE→git pusher'. Counterpart-agent paragraph added explaining dome-mirror handles the reverse direction.
workspace_sync_changes.step_4c_block_removal: The PROP-066 helper invocation block (FIRST-position per PROP-072), the PROP-073 stage-delta gating + commit block, and the PROP-073 sub-fix #2 filename-ISO SENTINEL_COUNT_THIS_CYCLE python block — all removed from the collapsed Step 4 bash block. Replaced with a brief comment noting the work moved to dome-mirror.
workspace_sync_changes.agent_notes_simplification: Removed `step4c-not-invoked` (PROP-072 self-attestation) and `step4c-mis-titled` (PROP-073 detective backstop) from AGENT_NOTES required-string list. These were detective layers for the now-removed Step 4c work.
preserved_unchanged: PROP-068 collapsed Step 4 architecture (Step 4a + capture + Step 4b + cleanup remain in ONE bash block; trap-protected; explicit final rm)
PROP-051 patches (degraded-mode prohibitions, disk-pressure pre-flight, working-tree-size check, delete-sanity gate)
[CLONE-DEBUG] and [CLONE-LEAK] verification logging at end of Step 4 (detective-only, still valuable)
Universal-pusher rescue path for runtime data files (data/, decision state, applied-patches)
PROP-045 anti-reversion content-hash check inside smart_copy
PROP-024 JSON-validity gate + PROP-050 mtime-drift backstop for docs/index.html

rules_section_updates.added: 'Do NOT run node build.js sync-workspace or monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js. That is the dome-mirror agent's job.'
Counterpart-agent rule replacing the 'Bidirectional sync (PROP-061)' rule. Explains dome-mirror handles git→FUSE; together the two agents cover both directions.
rules_section_updates.removed: Old 'Bidirectional sync (PROP-061)' rule that described workspace-sync as bidirectional.

operator_action_required.summary: Tinker authors PROP + prompts + workspace-sync changes; operator creates the scheduled task. dome-mirror is NOT running until operator registers it.

fields.description: Git→FUSE propagation: detect upstream commits not yet visible in the workspace FUSE mount, run monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js to propagate, commit + push the resulting sentinel. Companion to dome-workspace-sync (which handles the FUSE→git direction). PROP-074 architectural separation.
fields.model_hint: Haiku — single mechanical action; cost matters at hourly cadence. If reliability needs Sonnet, operator decides.
fields.skill_md_content:
---
name: dome-mirror
description: Git→FUSE propagation: detect upstream commits not yet in workspace FUSE mount, propagate via sync-workspace-step4c.js helper, commit + push the resulting sentinel.
---

Read your full instructions from the prompt file at `monitor/prompts/dome-mirror.md` in the "dome-model-review" folder in your workspace. Follow those instructions exactly — they contain your complete procedure, output schemas, and rules.

Your workspace contains the "dome-model-review" folder with all agent prompts, outputs, and configuration. Start by reading the dome-mirror prompt file, then execute it.

scheduled_task_creation_recipe.scheduling_offset_note: Recommend dome-mirror at `0 * * * *` and dome-workspace-sync moved to `5 * * * *` (or kept at its current hourly slot but verified ≥5 min after dome-mirror). dome-mirror updating FUSE before workspace-sync reads FUSE prevents the mtime-guard backfire where workspace-sync would otherwise revert recent commits.

validation.synthetic_test_planned: After this PROP commits, tinker runs the dome-mirror.md prompt's Step 1 + Step 2 bash blocks manually (without the scheduled-task wrapper). Expected: PAT prelude verifies HTTP 200, fresh clone succeeds, working-tree-size check passes, sync-workspace-step4c.js invokes successfully, a sentinel is written to monitor/integrity/sync-workspace-runs-*.json with action='no-op-no-upstream-movement' OR 'auto-sync' depending on origin state at test time, commit + push succeed, cleanup removes the clone.
validation.post_deploy_verification: After operator creates the scheduled task, expected behavior over the first 24h: ≥20/24 hourly cycles produce a sync-workspace-runs-*.json sentinel (≥83% sentinel rate, vs ~14% under the prior in-prompt architecture). The metric to watch: count of new sync-workspace-runs files per 24h window vs cron-cycle count.
validation.workspace_sync_regression_check: Verify workspace-sync.md still runs its FUSE→git half reliably after the simplification. Past sentinel rate for workspace-sync's own run-reports was ~100%; should remain so.

lessons_captured: Agent self-conception filters out unfamiliar actions regardless of prompt design. Extending an agent's responsibilities beyond its established identity triggers pattern-rejection failure modes that are NOT addressable via prompt design alone.

FIRST + LAST positions execute reliably across Sonnet/Haiku; MIDDLE positions drop. This was empirically established by PROP-072 but did not generalize to 'just put the new work at FIRST position' — pattern-rejection still fired against actions inconsistent with the agent's established role.

Self-attestation backstops are gameable. PROP-072/073's AGENT_NOTES `step4c-not-invoked` required the agent to NOTICE it didn't run the action; the same pattern-rejection that caused the skip also caused the agent to not notice it skipped. Detective signal requires structural evidence (sentinel files, commit titles, file mtimes), not LLM self-report.

Stage-delta detection (PROP-073 sub-fix #1) DID work for preventing mis-titled commits. Defense-in-depth detection layers DO work; preventive prompt-design fixes for pattern-rejection do not. This is the right architecture: prevent the structural failure mode with detection layers, fix the underlying pattern-rejection with architectural separation.

Architectural separation into single-purpose agents is the structural fix when extending a multi-purpose agent triggers the pattern-rejection bug class. The cost (one more scheduled task slot, one more hourly cycle of clone + push) is acceptable when the alternative is permanent unreliability.

PROP-066.preserved: monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js helper script — sole mechanism dome-mirror invokes; PROP-074 reuses as-is
PROP-066.obsolete: The in-workspace-sync Step 4c invocation block — removed by PROP-074

PROP-068.preserved: Collapse architecture for Step 4 (commit + capture + report + cleanup in ONE bash tool call; trap-protected; explicit final rm) — still in workspace-sync.md and remains the right structural fix for trailing-block skip
PROP-068.obsolete: The 'Step 4c FIRST position' aspect of the collapse — Step 4c is gone entirely from workspace-sync

PROP-072.rationale: Hoist-to-FIRST positioning was a fix for Step 4c's middle-position skip. With Step 4c removed from workspace-sync entirely, there's nothing to hoist. The empirical lesson (FIRST + LAST positions execute reliably) remains valuable for future prompt-design but the specific PROP-072 fix is obsolete.

PROP-073.rationale: Stage-delta gating, AGENT_NOTES self-attestation backstop, filename-ISO parse, and absolute write-path log were detective layers for the now-removed Step 4c work. With nothing to detect, the layers are obsolete. The helper script's absolute write-path log (sub-fix #3) remains in the helper itself and continues to provide value for dome-mirror runs.

PROP-061.preserved: git→FUSE direction is still done (now by dome-mirror) using node build.js sync-workspace via the helper. The direction itself isn't going away.
PROP-061.obsolete: The 'in-workspace-sync Step 4c' implementation approach — replaced by separate scheduled agent

PROP-064.preserved: PRE_PULL_SHA capture and PULL_MOVED signal concept — folded into helper's durable-state-derived NEED_SYNC computation

non_goals_observed: Did NOT delete monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js — preserved as PROP-074's mechanism
Did NOT delete PROP-066/068/072/073 proposal files — preserved as historical record (this PROP's iteration_history captures the lesson)
Did NOT touch PROP-071 (FUSE-staleness audit) — independent work, operator review pending
Did NOT bump cadences of other agents
Did NOT bump dome-mirror's model above Haiku — operator pre-baked answer per directive Q2
verification_pattern:
Three checks tinker (or any future audit) can run to verify PROP-074 is in effect:
  1. grep -c '^node monitor/scripts/sync-workspace-step4c.js' monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md → expect 0 (no INVOCATION lines remain; only REFERENTIAL mentions in counterpart-agent prose).
  2. test -f monitor/prompts/dome-mirror.md AND wc -l monitor/prompts/dome-mirror.md → expect file present and ≥150 lines.
  3. After operator creates the dome-mirror scheduled task, verify the post-deploy sentinel rate ≥83% (≥20 sync-workspace-runs-*.json files per 24h window of hourly dome-mirror cron cycles).
